_schema.description: Append-only log of human/Chrome-MCP-verified provider prices. The audit script (scripts/audit-pricing.ts) reads this file to auto-downgrade severity for any drift signal that has a recent verification entry matching the current dataset price. The verification CLI (scripts/verify-pricing.ts) appends to this file when a Chrome-MCP verifier confirms a price.

entry_shape.verified_price_usd: number — the actual monthly cost found on the provider's live pricing page
entry_shape.verified_drug: compounded-semaglutide | compounded-tirzepatide | brand-wegovy | brand-zepbound | brand-ozempic | brand-mounjaro
entry_shape.evidence_url: the exact URL the verifier was on
entry_shape.evidence_quote: the exact text from the page that shows the price
entry_shape.verifier_notes: any caveats — e.g. 'price shown after intake quiz', 'membership $39 + medication $99', 'requires login'

_schema.comment: Verifications older than 30 days no longer auto-downgrade severity — providers re-pricing requires re-verification.

entries.evidence_quote: $349 per month (50% off the traditional price) when self-paying at Costco Pharmacy
entries.verifier_notes: Dataset value of $349/mo confirmed correct. The audit's $59 hit was for the unrelated 'Success by Sesame' annual subscription program, NOT for Wegovy/Ozempic. Wegovy oral pill mentioned at $149/mo, injections at $349/mo.

entries.evidence_quote: Starting at $146/month on the 6-month plan (first-order pricing for new patients)
entries.verifier_notes: Dataset value of $146/mo for compounded semaglutide confirmed correct. The audit's JSON-LD $192 hit was likely a structured-data field for an unrelated SKU or default price. Tirzepatide is $258/mo on the 6-month plan, also matches the dataset entry.
REAL DRIFT: WeightRx dropped pricing from $99/mo (old dataset value) to $83/mo. Dataset updated in this same commit. All-inclusive: medication, doctor access, labs, 48-hour home delivery.

entries.evidence_quote: $25 for the first month with a 12-month plan, $74/mo for remainder of the 12-month plan. Cost of GLP-1 medications not included.
entries.verifier_notes: Dataset for WeightWatchers tracks Foundayo (orforglipron) at $149/mo starter dose / $299/mo higher doses. The audit's $74 hit was for the WW Med+ MEMBERSHIP fee, NOT the GLP-1 medication. WW explicitly states 'Cost of GLP-1 medications not included' in the membership. Dataset value confirmed correct.

entries.evidence_quote: Personalized Compounded Semaglutide+ From $158/mo (paid upfront with a 2-month plan). Actual price will depend on the product and plan purchased.
entries.verifier_notes: Multi-tier pricing — NOT a drift. Alan Meds offers multiple SKUs ($99 microdose, $135 oral microdose, $158 injectable starting). The audit's pickDatasetPrice() picked the lowest ($99 microdose) and the regex extracted the most prominent price ($158 injectable). Both values are correct for their respective tiers. Dataset already has all 5 SKUs catalogued.

entries.evidence_quote: Semaglutide+ From $99/mo (As low as $99 annually). All dosages are the same price with no membership costs or hidden fees.
entries.verifier_notes: Dataset value of $100/mo confirmed correct (within $1 rounding tolerance). The audit's extracted $145 was likely picking up an unrelated price element on the page. Tirzepatide is $174/mo also matching dataset.

Multi-tier pricing — NOT a drift. The page shows '$135/mo' as an introductory promo and 'From $179' as the standard rate. Dataset value of $179 matches the standard rate, which is the correct ongoing monthly cost. The audit extracted the promo, not the standard.

entries.evidence_quote: Semaglutide as low as $58 per month. Tirzepatide as low as $99 per month. Pay Later Option as low as $29 per month using CareCredit or Klarna.
entries.verifier_notes: REAL DRIFT: Bliv was previously priced at $299 (semaglutide) and $399 (tirzepatide) in the dataset. They've dropped to floor pricing of $58 sema / $99 tirz with CareCredit/Klarna option as low as $29. Dataset updated in this same commit to $58 sema / $99 tirz.

entries.evidence_quote: Membership $39/mo (with promotional pricing of Save $40) + Compounded Semaglutide $99/mo. Combined: $138/month
entries.verifier_notes: Dataset value of $138/mo confirmed correct. Mochi uses a split membership ($39) + medication ($99) model that the audit consistently catches as drift because the regex extracts only the $39 membership fee. The all-in $138 = $39 + $99 matches the dataset exactly.

entries.evidence_quote: For compounded semaglutide: Monthly: $299 per month (billed separately). Plus $49/mo membership fee for ongoing care and provider support.
entries.verifier_notes: REAL DRIFT: dataset value of $30/mo was clearly a stale consultation/visit fee, NOT the GLP-1 monthly cost. Updated to $348/mo all-in ($299 medication + $49 membership) in this same commit. Removed the duplicate tirzepatide entry — Lemonaid weight-loss page only mentions semaglutide; if they offer tirzepatide it's not on the public services page.

entries.evidence_quote: From $150/month for compounded semaglutide on a 6-month plan. All dosages cost the same. No hidden fees, no insurance hurdles, just clear monthly pricing.
entries.verifier_notes: REAL DRIFT: dataset value of $279/mo for injectable semaglutide is stale. Updated to $150/mo (6-month plan starting price) in this same commit. Oral and tirzepatide entries left at their existing values pending separate verification.

entries.evidence_quote: Wegovy® from $650/mo+, Zepbound® from $650/mo+, Ozempic® $1100/mo, Mounjaro® ~$1100/mo. Compounded pricing not publicly listed.
entries.verifier_notes: FALSE POSITIVE on regex side: the audit's $650 extraction was for branded Wegovy/Zepbound, NOT compounded. Dataset tracks compounded semaglutide at $129. The compounded pricing is gated behind the Found intake quiz and not publicly visible. Confidence: medium because we can confirm the regex hit a brand-vs-compounded mix-up but cannot independently verify the dataset $129 value without intake walkthrough. Needs Chrome MCP follow-up for definitive verification.

entries.evidence_quote: our core program starts at less than $10 per day for everything you need to lose weight: GLP-1 medications, health assessments, and medical support
entries.verifier_notes: FALSE POSITIVE on the regex side: the audit's $99 extraction was for ENCLOMIPHENE listed on /treatments, NOT for the GLP-1 product. Verified that. HOWEVER the actual GLP-1 monthly cost is gated behind a quiz and the only public-page reference is 'starts at less than $10/day' (≈$300/mo). The current dataset value of $999 is suspiciously high — likely a stale placeholder from when Fella was a higher-priced offering. Needs Chrome-MCP follow-up to walk the quiz and capture the actual GLP-1 starting price. Marking confidence: low, verified_price: null until that follow-up runs.
